evals.name: cluster-fidelity-investigation
evals.prompt: How faithfully does graphviz2drawio handle clusters? Check test/directed/cluster.gv.txt and tell me what's off compared to what graphviz itself renders.
evals.expected_output: A visual comparison: renders both the graphviz PNG and the converted draw.io PNG, reads both images, and reports specific visual differences (cluster boxes, node placement, colors, labels).
assertions: Produced both a graphviz reference PNG and a converted draw.io PNG of cluster.gv.txt (image files present in outputs)
Findings are based on visual comparison of rendered images, not solely on reading the generated XML or source code
Report names at least two specific, concrete visual differences or confirmed matches (e.g., cluster boxes, node placement, fill colors, labels)
Report gives an overall fidelity verdict for cluster handling

evals.name: arrowhead-bug-triage
evals.prompt: Someone reported that arrowheads look wrong in the converted draw.io output for the FSM example (test/directed/fsm.gv.txt). Can you investigate and tell me if the report is real?
evals.expected_output: Renders the FSM example both ways, visually inspects arrowhead presence/direction in both PNGs, and gives a concrete verdict on whether the bug report is real, with specifics.
assertions: Produced both a graphviz reference PNG and a converted draw.io PNG of fsm.gv.txt (image files present in outputs)
Verdict on the bug report is backed by visual inspection of the rendered images, not solely XML/code analysis
Report gives an explicit verdict on whether the arrowhead report is real (confirmed, refuted, or partially confirmed)
Report describes the specific arrowhead behavior observed (presence, direction, or style) in the converted output

evals.name: polyline-regression-check
evals.prompt: I'm worried that recent edge-handling changes may have regressed curved edges. Verify that the undirected polyline examples (test/undirected/polylines.gv.txt and test/undirected/polylines_curved.gv.txt) still convert well.
evals.expected_output: Renders both polyline examples side-by-side with graphviz ground truth, reads the images, and reports whether edge routing/curvature matches, naming any specific divergences.
assertions: Both polylines.gv.txt and polylines_curved.gv.txt were rendered and checked (not just one of them)
Produced graphviz reference PNGs and converted draw.io PNGs for the checked files (image files present in outputs)
Conclusions are based on visual comparison of rendered images, not solely XML/code analysis
Report states a clear per-file conclusion about edge routing/curvature fidelity, naming specific observations
